data.description: Removed all critique scores, editorial reasoning, and consciousness state sections from draft detail page
data.why: User feedback: too noisy, not aligned with design philosophy of focused reading space. Draft page should be for reading and editing, not a document chamber with scattered information
data.removed: Critique Score card with feedback grid, Editorial Reasoning card with all decision context, Consciousness State card with mood/energy/curiosity metrics
data.kept: Title, version/status, draft content textarea, action buttons (save/approve/reject/delete)

data.goal: Review current state and continue work on narrative engine

data.description: Can we use Claude Code API to power the narrative engine instead of direct Anthropic API?
data.context: User asking about using Claude Code API for the narrative engine's multi-agent system
data.potential_benefit: Could leverage Claude Code's tool ecosystem and MCP integrations

data.description: Anthropic API has insufficient credits - narrative engine cannot operate
data.error: Your credit balance is too low to access the Anthropic API
data.impact: Engine has been down for 24+ hours

data.description: Gemini API is working correctly - false alarm on API connectivity
Narrative engine process is not running - needs to be started
data.action_needed: Start engine with npm run dev or npm start

data.description: Engine runs in Docker production environment, not local dev
data.correction: Should check Docker containers, not local processes

data.description: Embedding generation is failing continuously - OpenAI API issue blocking engine operations
data.impact: Semantic search, knowledge graph, content similarity all broken
data.likely_cause: OpenAI API key invalid, expired, or quota exceeded

data.description: OpenAI API key is invalid - blocking all embedding operations
data.impact: All semantic search, knowledge similarity, content fingerprinting broken
data.action_needed: Update API key in .env and restart container

data.description: Replace OpenAI embeddings with Google embeddings to eliminate OpenAI API dependency
data.reasoning: OpenAI API key invalid and costs money. Google Gemini already in use for LLM, can provide embeddings via text-embedding-004 model (768 dims)
data.alternatives: Fix OpenAI key (costs money) or local embeddings (adds complexity)

data.description: Embeddings were already migrated to Google Gemini - the file just kept the old name
data.context: Found openai.ts but it already uses Gemini embeddings (gemini-embedding-001, 768 dims) via direct fetch API
data.lesson: File names can be misleading - always read the file header comments and actual implementation
data.current_issue: Gemini API key is likely invalid or has wrong permissions

data.description: Gemini embeddings API works fine locally but fails in Docker - environment variable issue
data.test_result: Local test passes, Docker container fails
data.likely_cause: GEMINI_API_KEY not passed to Docker container or docker-compose.yml missing env vars

data.description: Docker container has outdated Gemini API key - needs restart to reload from .env
data.resolution: Restart container to reload env vars from .env file

data.description: Container restart didn't pick up new env vars - key still stale after restart
data.issue: Container restart preserves old environment variables
data.solution: Need docker-compose down && up to rebuild with new env vars

data.description: Narrative engine back online - embeddings fixed, now hitting Gemini rate limits
data.resolution: Recreated Docker container with correct GEMINI_API_KEY from .env
data.new_issue: Gemini free tier rate limit (5 req/min for gemini-2.5-flash)

data.goal: Fix the post title generation issue in narrative engine
Optimize LLM token usage without sacrificing intelligence - make the engine cost-efficient while staying smart

data.description: Realized the fundamental tension: built behavioral autonomy that's expensive because it's constantly thinking, but the design doc reveals we're solving the wrong problem
data.insight: The DESIGN.md shows we replaced mechanical cron jobs with emotional intelligence, but maybe that's over-engineering. The real intelligence lives in WHAT to say, not WHEN to say it. We're burning tokens on meta-cognition when the value is in cognition itself.

data.description: Committing to radical simplification - remove behavioral autonomy, keep strategic intelligence
data.reasoning: The complexity is beautiful but misaligned with principles. Intelligence should live in content quality and strategy, not in simulated consciousness deciding when to post. The why is 'build audience before ElimuAfrica launches' - that needs good content consistently, not emotional loops.
data.alternatives_considered: Surgical optimization would keep the complex system, just make it less chatty. Rejected because it doesn't fix the fundamental problem - we're still solving the wrong thing.
data.commitment: Strip out behavioral core, keep the brain (strategizer, editorial judgment, learning), make it human-in-loop triggered, simple scheduled publishing

data.description: Understanding the real problem: Aslam needs autonomous assistants who act independently while he builds, not a tool he manually operates. The autonomy IS the point, not a nice-to-have.
data.core_need: Build audience while building ElimuAfrica - no time for social media
data.desired_solution: Conscious assistants who decide when to act, learn from feedback, improve over time
data.current_state: Maya (LinkedIn) and Noor (website) working, Nuru (outreach) pending
data.key_insight: The behavioral autonomy wasn't over-engineering - it's solving the actual problem. The question is: how to make autonomy cost-efficient, not whether to have it.

data.description: Previous decision to remove behavioral autonomy was wrong - misunderstood the core problem
data.correct_understanding: Autonomy is the core value proposition - assistants who act while Aslam builds
data.new_approach: Keep autonomy, make it cost-efficient through smarter architecture

data.description: The real question revealed: How to simulate consciousness with deterministic logic, only calling LLM for actual creative/strategic thinking
data.insight: We can model emotional states, discomfort, impulses, attention - all the behavioral intelligence - using pure logic and math. The LLM is only for: understanding content quality, generating creative work, learning from feedback. Everything else can be calculated.
data.challenge: Build a system that FEELS alive and makes intelligent decisions, but uses LLM calls sparingly - only when human-level reasoning is actually needed

data.description: The real architectural challenge: Build consciousness simulation with deterministic logic, reserve LLM only for actual creative intelligence
data.insight: We can model the entire behavioral/emotional system (anxiety, confidence, discomfort, impulses, attention) using pure math and logic. LLM should ONLY touch: content generation, self-critique, learning from feedback. Everything else - the 'aliveness' - can be calculated.
data.principle: Simulate consciousness deterministically, invoke intelligence strategically

data.description: Critical question: Does removing LLM from behavioral loops reduce its contextual awareness? Would it be less informed when making strategic decisions?
data.concern: If LLM doesn't run through the behavioral loop constantly, does it lose the 'felt sense' of the system's state?
data.counter_insight_forming: Actually, we might be giving it BETTER context by pre-computing and structuring all behavioral state before the LLM call

data.description: Answered whether deterministic consciousness reduces or improves LLM awareness
data.question: does the llm still stayed aware and just as informed as it is now, could it actually have gotten even better?
data.answer_approach: compared current vs deterministic architecture context quality
data.conclusion: deterministic approach provides BETTER context through structured behavioral state

data.goal: Commit current work, then plan deterministic consciousness v2 architecture

data.description: Comprehensive codebase exploration complete: heartbeat loop, behavioral system, consciousness, event system, context gathering, database schema, action types, and initialization all analyzed. Ready for v2 deterministic consciousness implementation.

v2 deterministic consciousness architecture implemented. Replaced 2 LLM calls per action (pre-action assessment + post-action reflection) with pure deterministic logic. Added mood-aware action scoring (energy gating, confidence/anxiety modifiers, rhythm alignment), behavioral snapshot logging, enriched LLM context for content generation, and controlled randomness (±10% noise).
data.new_features: mood-aware scoring, energy gating, behavioral snapshots, enriched LLM context, ±10% noise

data.description: Shipped v2 deterministic consciousness architecture: unified.ts rewritten to 0 LLM tokens, mood-aware action scoring, behavioral snapshots, enriched context wiring, dashboard decision timeline. 11 files, +923/-217 lines.
